description_long: This bungalow consists of two main structures, both featuring simple, rectangular forms with gable roofs clad in weathered brown shingles. The larger section has an L-shaped footprint, with a prominent roof intersection and several window openings on its visible facades. The smaller section is a detached rectangular building with a matching gable roof and three front-facing window and door openings. The exterior walls are clad in horizontal wooden siding, giving the buildings a rustic, utilitarian appearance. The windows and doors are unadorned, with no visible trim or decorative elements. The overall design is functional and modest, suitable for suburban or rural residential settings.
description: A pair of simple, brown wooden bungalows with gable roofs and rectangular forms, featuring multiple window and door openings. The structures have a rustic, utilitarian appearance with horizontal siding and minimal ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view showing both the L-shaped main bungalow and the smaller detached building, with visible window and door openings on both structures.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the intersecting gable roofs and the depth of the L-shaped main building, with the smaller building partially obscured behind.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the full roof layouts, including the cross-gabled intersection on the main bungalow and the simple gable on the smaller structure.
description_view_3: Rear view of the main bungalow, showing two window openings and the extended length of the building, with the smaller structure aligned behind.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical with multiple rectangular windows on front and side facades
